name: Leafy Seadragon
primary_sources: Australian government's Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999

visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: leaf-like appendages distributed along the body

domain_specific_visual.FIN_PLACEMENT: pectoral fins on neck, dorsal fin near tail

domain_specific_functional.FEEDING_MECHANISM: sucks up prey through long snout

relationships.description: Both are members of the family Syngnathidae and share similar habitats.
Shares family Syngnathidae and similar reproductive traits.

contextual_attributes.origin: southern and western coasts of Australia
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: from Wilson's Promontory in Victoria to Jurien Bay in Western Australia
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of marine conservation and emblem of South Australia
primary_sources: Australian government's Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999
